Forza Milan



2017/2018球季意甲賽程：

常見問題：
AC米蘭同國際米蘭又係有"米蘭"，應該點分？
ANS：AC米蘭簡稱米蘭，國際米蘭叫國米，避免稱呼做AC，因為AC係球會咁解，而milan先係名既簡稱。
AC米蘭仲係咪「老人院」？
ANS：現時全隊平均年齡為25歲。
今個轉會窗到目前為止簽咗咁多球員，遲啲仲會唔會有錢買人？
ANS：其實買球員好少會一炮過畀，多數都係分期，所以仲會有錢簽其他球員。
簽咗咁多球員會唔會過唔到FFP？
ANS：早前米蘭管理層有同UEFA探討經營計劃。
http://sempremilan.com/ts-fassone-meets-uefa-to-discuss-ac-milan-business-plan（By 二石四鳥)

Abbiati: 'Reina and Callejon great'

Milan club manager Christian Abbiati does nothing to deny reports Pepe Reina and even Jose Callejon could join from Napoli.

“As long as it is mathematically possible, we will continue to believe in a Champions League place, but meanwhile we need to defend our spot in the Europa League,” the club manager told Mediaset Premium.

“This is a difficult match today, but we know what we’re capable of and will fight it out on even terms with Napoli.”

Pepe Reina has reportedly already agreed terms with Milan to move in June, when he’ll be a free agent, raising questions on the future of Gianluigi Donnarumma.

“They are both great goalkeepers. I compliment Gigio for reaching 100 Serie A appearances. Reina has a lot of experience and won a great deal in his career, so it’ll be an interesting duel today.”

There were also reports this week of a potential exchange deal between Milan and Napoli for wingers Suso and Jose Callejon.

“Callejon is a fine player, but we have Suso and I’m holding on tight to him. Of course, if Callejon were to come to Milan as well, that’d be even better.”

Abbiati was also asked about Juventus captain Gigi Buffon’s tirade against referee Michael Oliver after a Champions League exit to Real Madrid.

“His rant is understandable and justifiable in part. Going out at the last second to a penalty like that isn’t easy to swallow, so he should be understood in the circumstances. I do think when Gigi watches the interview back, he’ll realise that he went over the line.”

“This is a difficult match today, but we know what we’re capable of and will fight it out on even terms with Napoli.”

Pepe Reina has reportedly already agreed terms with Milan to move in June, when he’ll be a free agent, raising questions on the future of Gianluigi Donnarumma.

“They are both great goalkeepers. I compliment Gigio for reaching 100 Serie A appearances. Reina has a lot of experience and won a great deal in his career, so it’ll be an interesting duel today.”

There were also reports this week of a potential exchange deal between Milan and Napoli for wingers Suso and Jose Callejon.

“Callejon is a fine player, but we have Suso and I’m holding on tight to him. Of course, if Callejon were to come to Milan as well, that’d be even better.”

Abbiati was also asked about Juventus captain Gigi Buffon’s tirade against referee Michael Oliver after a Champions League exit to Real Madrid.

“His rant is understandable and justifiable in part. Going out at the last second to a penalty like that isn’t easy to swallow, so he should be understood in the circumstances. I do think when Gigi watches the interview back, he’ll realise that he went over the line.”

The player, however, failed to impress, and Gattuso did nothing to hide his disappointment at the end of the game. “I tried to play Suso as an attacking midfielder but sometimes you also need to see how a player reacts to something new. Gattuso had previously talked to Premium Sport saying: “We did suffer in the last 20 minutes, we allowed a goal because there was a mistake by Abate who lost his man. According to Tuttosport, Milan sporting director Massimiliano Mirabelli was offered Sokratis by various intermediaries.

The Greece international is no stranger to the Rossoneri, having played seven times for the club during the 2010-11 campaign.

However, he failed to displace the likes of Alessandro Nesta and Thiago Silva and went on to join Werder Bremen, before Dortmund in the summer of 2013.

Sokratis’ contract with BVB expires next year, but the newspaper notes he is ready to leave the club once the season is out.

On the other hand, the Diavolo would not be able to afford the €15m that Dortmund are ‘at least’ holding out for.
